data.title: Grayscale transferred 1628 ETH and nearly 265 BTC to Coinbase 10 hours ago.
data.description: According to Arkham, Grayscale transferred 1,628 ETH ($3.29 million) and 264.974 BTC ($18.31 million) to a Coinbase Prime address approximately 10 hours ago.

data.title: Blue Owl Leads $750 Million Debt Financing for Nexthink Buyout
data.description: Blue Owl Capital has spearheaded a $750 million debt financing initiative for Vista Equity Partners' acquisition of software company Nexthink. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing interest in private credit despite prevailing market concerns over liquidity and potential disruptions from AI advancements. This move underscores the resilience of private credit markets amid broader economic uncertainties.

data.title: A whale pledged 8,208 ETH after a year of dormancy, worth $16.85 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale staked 8,208 ETH, worth $16.85 million, after a year of dormancy. This whale spent $16.09 million to accumulate these ETH over the past four years, but ultimately only made a profit of about $768,000.

data.title: On Polymarket, the probability of "crude oil prices breaking $100 in March" has surged to 76%.
data.description: Data from Polymarket shows that the probability of crude oil prices breaking $100 in March surged from a low of 35% yesterday to 76%, with trading volume exceeding $10 million. Previously, JPMorgan Chase stated that even a mere 3-4 weeks of disruption to passage through the Strait of Hormuz could force Gulf Cooperation Council member states to shut down oil production and push Brent crude prices above $100 per barrel.

data.title: Prediction Market Platforms Seek New Funding Rounds Amid High Valuation Goals
data.description: Prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket are reportedly engaging in discussions with potential investors to secure new funding rounds. According to NS3.AI, both companies are targeting valuations in the hundreds of millions of dollars. The negotiations are described as ongoing, and the funding rounds have not yet been finalized.

data.title: Kaito AI Launches Beta Version of Kaito Studio with New Platform Model
data.description: Kaito AI has unveiled the beta version of its Kaito Studio, marking the adoption of a new platform model starting today. According to NS3.AI, the project has also introduced an initial group of 16 partners. However, specific details regarding the new model have not been disclosed.

data.title: BlackRock's $26 billion private credit fund restricts redemptions, raising market concerns about spillover pressure onto Bitcoin and DeFi.
data.description: A private lending fund managed by asset management giant BlackRock, with approximately $26 billion in assets under management, has begun restricting withdrawals due to rising redemption requests, raising concerns about the spillover of pressure from the global private lending market. Analysts warn that the risk could also be transmitted directly on-chain. Data shows that the current scale of on-chain private lending is approaching $5 billion, mainly entering DeFi in the form of RWA tokens. Once the underlying credit assets experience impairment or default, the net value fluctuations of the related tokens could trigger liquidations or liquidity tightening, thereby transmitting traditional credit pressure to the DeFi ecosystem. In addition, tensions in this sector could be transmitted to the crypto market through both macro deleveraging and tokenized lending products. If private lending funds are forced to deleverage or liquidate assets, it could trigger a chain reaction in a wider range of risky assets, affecting crypto assets including Bitcoin. (CoinDesk)

data.title: U.S. Draft Rules Propose Broad Use of AI Models in Civil Government Contracts
data.description: The United States is considering new draft regulations that would permit the use of artificial intelligence (AI) models for any lawful purpose within civil government contracts. According to Jin10, this proposal aims to expand the flexibility and application of AI technologies in government operations. The draft rules are part of a broader effort to integrate advanced technologies into public sector functions, potentially enhancing efficiency and innovation. The move reflects a growing recognition of AI's potential to transform various aspects of governance and public service delivery. The proposal is still under review and subject to further discussion and refinement before any implementation.

data.title: Pardon Seekers Allegedly Charged Over $1 Million for Access to Trump
data.description: Individuals seeking presidential pardons are reportedly being charged over $1 million by lobbyists and lawyers who claim to have access to U.S. President Donald Trump. New York Times posted on X, highlighting that these intermediaries often promise bonuses if they successfully secure a pardon. This practice has raised ethical concerns about the influence of money in the presidential pardon process. Critics argue that it undermines the integrity of the justice system, as those with financial means may have an unfair advantage in obtaining clemency. The situation has prompted calls for greater transparency and reform in the pardon process to ensure fairness and accountability.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Holdings Decrease by 2.573 Tons
data.description: The world's largest gold exchange-traded fund, SPDR Gold Trust, has reported a reduction in its holdings. According to Jin10, the fund's holdings decreased by 2.573 tons, bringing the current total to 1,073.321 tons. This change in holdings reflects ongoing market dynamics and investor sentiment towards gold as a safe-haven asset. The adjustment in SPDR Gold Trust's holdings is closely watched by market participants as it can influence gold prices and market trends.

data.title: OpenAI and Oracle may abandon plans to expand their large AI data center in Texas.
data.description: OpenAI and Oracle have decided to abandon plans to expand a large artificial intelligence data center in Texas. Negotiations between the two companies regarding the project had stalled for a long time due to financing issues and continuously changing needs of OpenAI. Sources familiar with the matter said that the breakdown of negotiations created an opportunity for Meta to intervene. Meta is currently considering leasing the data center site in Abilene, Texas, originally intended for expansion, from developer Crusoe. Nvidia reportedly played a role in facilitating Meta's contact with the developer. (Reuters)

data.title: Kalshi and Polymarket are both seeking funding at valuations of approximately $20 billion.
data.description: Prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket are reportedly in talks with potential investors for new funding rounds, each targeting a valuation of approximately $20 billion. Sources familiar with the matter indicate that the two companies were valued at about half that level at the end of last year. As competition intensifies in the prediction market, both companies are accelerating their efforts to grow their user base and use this momentum to advance these new funding negotiations. (Wall Street Journal)

data.title: Oil Prices Surge Amid Persian Gulf Conflict
data.description: Oil markets experienced significant turbulence this week due to the outbreak of war in the Persian Gulf, a region responsible for approximately 20% of the global oil supply. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the rapid increase in gasoline prices across the United States as a direct consequence of the conflict. The escalation in the Persian Gulf has raised concerns about potential disruptions in oil supply, leading to heightened volatility in global energy markets. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation, as any prolonged conflict could further impact oil prices and economic stability worldwide. The U.S. market is particularly sensitive to these developments, given its reliance on oil imports from the region. As the situation unfolds, stakeholders are urged to stay informed about potential shifts in the energy landscape.

data.title: Kaito AI: Kaito Studio beta version is now live, with 16 initial partners joining.
data.description: Kaito AI announced on the X platform the official launch of the Kaito Studio beta version. Starting today, the platform adopts a new model, allowing brands and creators to be matched more effectively based on fit, preferences, and expectations. The initial lineup includes 16 partners, with more to be added as details are finalized. Previously, the X platform revised its developer API policy, no longer allowing applications that offer rewards to users for posting on the X platform (i.e., InfoFi).

data.title: Bitcoin Risk Index May Drop to 25 Amid ETF Inflows
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Swissblock has reported that its Bitcoin Risk Index is showing an inverse relationship with spot Bitcoin ETF fund flows. The firm notes that if inflows continue, the Risk Index could potentially fall to 25 or lower. Swissblock observes that ETF outflows are associated with an unstable Risk Index and significant selling pressure. Conversely, ETF inflows are linked to declines in the Risk Index, a pattern that has been consistent since November and became more pronounced last week.

data.title: Jim Bianco Questions Economic Measurement Application
data.description: Jim Bianco, president of Bianco Research posted on X, raises questions about the application of new methods to measure the economy. He suggests that advancements could soon impact economic analysis. Bianco's inquiry reflects ongoing discussions about integrating innovative approaches in economic assessments. The potential shift in measurement techniques could redefine how economic data is interpreted and utilized.

data.title: Pakistani Man Convicted in Plot to Assassinate U.S. Politicians
data.description: A federal jury has convicted a Pakistani man for conspiring with the Iranian government to assassinate U.S. politicians, including President Donald Trump. New York Times posted on X, highlighting the gravity of the charges and the international implications of the plot. The verdict underscores ongoing geopolitical tensions and the potential threats posed by foreign entities targeting American leaders. The case has drawn significant attention due to its international dimension and the involvement of high-profile political figures. Further details about the plot and the individuals involved are expected to emerge as the case progresses.

data.title: Colombian Inflation Declines Unexpectedly in February
data.description: Colombian inflation saw an unexpected decline in February, contrary to analysts' predictions. Bloomberg posted on X that experts had anticipated a rise in consumer prices due to this year's record increase in the minimum wage. The unexpected cooling of inflation suggests that the anticipated pressure on consumer prices did not materialize as expected.
